medical_history.chief_complaint: abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, decreased ileostomy output
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient presents with obstructive symptoms after waking up from sleep with intense mid-abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, and low ileostomy output (~100cc for the day) and very little gas in the bag. Denies fever/chills/dizziness. Previously admitted for CHF exacerbation managed with diuresis.
medical_history.past_medical_history: 1. Hypertension, dyslipidemia, diabetes 2. ___ disease S/P bowel resection and colostomy now ileostomy, obesity, insulin-dependent Type 2 diabetes, osteoarthritis, persistent atrial fibrillation, cervical cancer, GERD, cystic pancreatic lesion, goiter and thyroid mass, pulmonary lesions, portal vein clot, anxiety, left tibia/fibula fracture with hardware
medical_history.family_history: Father with UC and esophageal cancer, maternal aunt with ___, mother with ___ and squamous cell carcinoma, grandmother developed AF at ___ years of age, maternal grandmother with lung cancer, cousin diagnosed with IBD at age ___

physical_examination.general_examination: Vitals: Afebrile, hemodynamically stable. A&O, well-appearing female in no apparent distress. Multiple well-healed surgical scars.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Abdomen soft, non-tender, non-distended, no rebound or guarding, stoma pink and productive of stool/gas, no edema in extremities.
